cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the father did when he was laid off. The options are (A) angry with his boss, (B) didn't care, (C) couldn't accept the fact easily, or (D) as happy as usual. First, I need to go back to the story and find the relevant parts. The passage says the father was laid off and came home. The son initially thought he was joking but then realized he was serious. The father's expression showed he was down, and losing the job affected his self-esteem. He tried to be optimistic but was clearly struggling. He started searching for jobs, asked the kids to spend less, and eventually decided to start his own business. So the key points here are that the father was affected emotionally, tried to be positive, and took action. The question is about his immediate reaction to being laid off. The son noticed his expression and realized he was telling the truth, which implies the father was serious and not joking. The father's behavior (searching for jobs, changes in the household) shows he was taking it seriously. Looking at the options, (A) angry with his boss—there's no mention of anger towards the boss. The text doesn't say he was angry. (B) Didn't care—no, because he was actively looking for jobs and making changes. (C) Couldn't accept the fact easily—this seems likely because he was down, affected self-esteem, and took time to adjust. (D) Happy as usual—definitely not, since he was laid off and it had negative effects. So the answer should be (C) couldn't accept the fact easily.
